guide_review_response.verdict: ITEM_2_10_VERIFIED_CLEAN_MECHANICALLY -- but 5 substantive narrative errors in the Stage 2 Closure Footnotes section require correction via 2.10.1 follow-on commit BEFORE closing handoff. Stage 2 NOT YET fully closed.
guide_review_response.work_item: 2.10 STATUS append verification + narrative-error inventory + corrections authorization

reviewing.ea_artifact: EA-6 chat report at Item 2.10 close (commit 89213ee)

guide_review_response.summary:
Independent disk verification confirms the 2.10 commit (89213ee) landed mechanically clean: parent 3d1ac35 preserved; 2 files in diff (STATUS.md +59/-5 = +54 net; Guide-#6 verdict XML +465 = NEW); STATUS.md grew 192 → 246 lines (NOT 139 → 176 as EA-6's commit message claimed -- absolute counts are off but the +59/-5 line-change delta IS correct per git show --stat); working tree clean post-commit; stash@{0} byte-identical (10-cycle preservation: v3 → v4 → Guide-5/6 → v5 → 2.7 → 2.7.v2 → docstring/2.8 → 2.9 → 2.10); state.json invariants preserved verbatim (last_updated_by="copilot_agent", last_updated_utc="2026-04-24T20:46:12Z"); all 12 cumulative footnote items present in the new "Stage 2 Closure Footnotes" section; A11 anomaly appended; Stage Ledger Stage 2 row PENDING → COMPLETE; append-only discipline preserved (A1-A10 anomalies untouched; Stage 0/1 ledger rows untouched; Recovery Anchors / Vikunja Project IDs sections untouched; prior Execution Log entries untouched).
HOWEVER, byte-level reading of the 12 Stage 2 Closure Footnotes section reveals 5 substantive narrative errors -- 2 HIGH severity, 2 MED severity, 1 LOW. Each is a factual misattribution that future maintainers reading STATUS.md (the LIVING STATE FILE per its own header) would find misleading. The errors do NOT affect: - the 2.10 commit's mechanical correctness - the audit anchor (disposition files in temp_for_responses/ + ea_outputs/, which ARE preserved verbatim and authoritative) - Stage 2 substantive work (commit 3d1ac35 is unchanged) But they DO affect: - audit-chain narrative accuracy (STATUS.md is supposed to be source-of-truth for "what happened at each stage") - g6-ea6_n7/n8/n9 verbatim-text obligation (the acks specified verbatim footnote text; EA-6 paraphrased and introduced errors during paraphrase)
DISPOSITION: AUTHORIZE EA-6 to apply 5 narrative corrections via a follow-on 2.10.1 commit (NOT --amend per no-amend doctrine on already-committed work). The corrections are small (~5 paragraphs of replaced text in the Stage 2 Closure Footnotes section). After 2.10.1 lands, EA-6 emits the closing handoff to LA per the closing_handoff_to_la_at_stage_2_close block. Then Stage 2 truly CLOSES.
Has EA-6 met "mature not minimal" through Item 2.10? EMPHATICALLY YES on the structural axes (12-item footnote presence + ledger row update + A11 anomaly + commit mechanics + invariant preservation through 10-cycle stash). Falls short on the narrative-accuracy axis, but the corrections are easily applicable and don't undo any structural achievement. EA-6's track record across the full Item 2.6+ phase remains excellent; this is a tactical narrative-paraphrase error, not a strategic discipline failure.
The closing handoff timing question: EA-6 asked "shall I (a) emit closing handoff now, or (b) wait for LA acknowledgment first?" Guide-#6 answer: NEITHER directly -- apply 2.10.1 narrative corrections FIRST, THEN emit closing handoff. The corrections are pre-requisite to a clean closing handoff (the closing handoff narrative summarizes Stage 2 work; EA-6 should not emit a summary built on incorrect footnote attributions).

independent_disk_verification.check: git log --oneline -4: 89213ee docs(platform-separation): Stage 2 close STATUS append + Guide-#6 verdict archive (Item 2.10) 3d1ac35 chore(platform_separation): Stage 2 multi-project refactor (DEC-15 platform extraction) f863a22 docs(platform_separation): Stage 2 mid-stage EA handoff v5 ... 9277d61 docs(platform_separation): Guide instance 5 to 6 handoff -- Stage 2 mid-stage at item 2.6 89213ee is the successor of 3d1ac35 ✓. Parent preserved. Subject line clear.

git show --stat 89213ee tail: docs/platform_separation/STATUS.md | 64 ++- ..._VERIFIED_ARTIFACT_GUIDANCE_PROCEED_TO_2_10.xml | 465 +++++++++++++++++++++ 2 files changed, 524 insertions(+), 5 deletions(-) ✓ Two files: STATUS.md (modified, 64 lines changed) + Guide-#6 verdict XML for Item 2.9 (NEW, 465 lines added). Math checks: 524 = 64 - 5 + 465.

git show 3d1ac35:docs/platform_separation/STATUS.md | wc -l → 192 (pre-commit) wc -l docs/platform_separation/STATUS.md → 246 (post-commit) Delta: +54 lines net. git show --stat 89213ee says STATUS.md got +59 / -5 = +54 net. ✓ Math matches.
EA-6's commit message body claim: "STATUS.md (139 -> 176 lines, +59 / -5)". - Pre count claimed: 139 (actual: 192; off by +53) - Post count claimed: 176 (actual: 246; off by +70) - Delta claimed: +59/-5 (correct) EA-6's absolute line counts are wrong; the delta (+59/-5) is correct. This is a documentation defect in the commit message, but NOT a content defect in STATUS.md itself. Severity: LOW. Acceptable as is (no need to amend the commit).
git status --porcelain post-commit: empty output ✓ V1 closure assertion satisfied (BlarAI git status clean post-Stage-2-commit + post-Stage-2-close STATUS append).

git stash list: stash@{0}: On main: wake_launcher auto-stash sdo 20260424_013002 -- byte-identical. 10-cycle preservation now (v3 → v4 → Guide-5/6 → v5 → 2.7 → 2.7.v2 → docstring/2.8 → 2.9 → 2.10). The cleanest single-axis discipline win in the procedure is now 10 cycles deep.

fleet_paused=true; reason "platform separation v2 in progress"; last_updated_by="copilot_agent"; last_updated_utc="2026-04-24T20:46:12Z" -- preserved verbatim through the entire 2.5-day procedure cycle. NEVER state.resume_fleet called.

Read docs/platform_separation/STATUS.md (246 lines). Structural elements all present: - Current State table (lines 30-41): updated for Stage 2 close ✓ * Active branch (BlarAI): chore/platform-extraction @ 3d1ac35 ✓ * Last completed stage: Stage 2 ✓ * Next stage: Stage 3 ✓ * Vikunja server line: updated to reflect g6-ea6_n8 Option B ✓ - Stage Ledger row Stage 2 (line 50): PENDING → COMPLETE with date 2026-04-26, commit 3d1ac35, evidence summary ✓ - Anomalies A1-A11 (lines 113-192): A1-A10 untouched ✓; A11 appended at lines 186-192 ✓ - Stage 2 Closure Footnotes section (NEW; lines 196-237): all 12 items present ✓ - Execution Log entries (lines 100-101): Stage 2 multi-EA summary + Stage 2 close entry appended ✓ - Recovery Anchors (lines 71-83): UNTOUCHED ✓ - Vikunja Project IDs (lines 58-67): UNTOUCHED ✓ - How to Resume After Interruption (lines 240-247): UNTOUCHED ✓ Append-only discipline strictly preserved. The 12-item cumulative footnote set per ack g5-10 + g6-ea6_n7/n8/n9 is structurally complete.
Byte-level reading of the 12 Stage 2 Closure Footnotes vs the actual disposition files reveals 5 substantive narrative errors. Detailed in <narrative_error_inventory> below.

error.ea_6_text: "Guide-#5 disposition (file `2.4-GUIDE_RESPONSE_INSTANCE_5_*` series): EA-3 had authored two near-duplicate `tools/tests/test_project_context.py` test functions that exercised the same code path with different fixture wiring. Disposition: collapse to one test; the higher-coverage variant retained. Closed at EA-4 Stage 2.5."
error.actual: Per Guide-#5 disposition file 2.4-GUIDE_RESPONSE_INSTANCE_5_VERIFICATION_OF_EA_4_F1_AND_2.5_WITH_ADJUSTMENTS.xml + Guide-#6 init pointer §v4_documentation_corrections_carried_forward D1: g5-7 was "duplicate test cleanup" -- specifically deletion of lines 76-94 of `tools/tests/test_vikunja_client_scope.py` (the verbatim duplicate copy of test_require_project_id_rejects_zero + test_require_project_id_rejects_negative). EA-4 (NOT EA-3) introduced the duplicates inadvertently during the f1 fix + cleanup-C3 work. EA-4 then deleted the duplicates per Guide-#5 disposition. Closed at EA-4 Item 2.5 corrections cycle (NOT "Stage 2.5" -- that's a different item; the actual item was 2.5 corrections after Item 2.4 partial work).
error.correction_required: Replace footnote 2 with: "Guide-#5 disposition file `2.4-GUIDE_RESPONSE_INSTANCE_5_VERIFICATION_OF_EA_4_F1_AND_2.5_WITH_ADJUSTMENTS.xml`: EA-4 inadvertently introduced two pairs of duplicate test definitions in `tools/tests/test_vikunja_client_scope.py` (test_require_project_id_rejects_zero + test_require_project_id_rejects_negative, each duplicated at lines 76-94). Disposition: delete the duplicate copy block at lines 76-94. Closed at EA-4 corrections cycle following Guide-#5's APPROVED_WITH_ADJUSTMENTS verdict."

error.ea_6_text: "During EA-4 → EA-5 transition, one PS1 helper was inadvertently written as UTF-8-BOM where the Stage 2 convention is UTF-8 (no BOM). Caught by Guide-#5 byte-level diff at v4 quality review. Re-emitted without BOM. No code-behavior change; cosmetic-but-procedural fix per dn5."
error.actual: Per Guide-#5 disposition file 2.4-GUIDE_RESPONSE_INSTANCE_5_F1_AND_2.5_CORRECTIONS_APPROVED.xml (commendation "encoding_regression_self_caught"): EA-4 self-caught and fixed the UTF-8 BOM regression in la_merge_approve.ps1 within the SAME corrections-cycle iteration. Guide-#5 commended EA-4's proactive catch: "Both modified comment blocks use 'Section 3' (ASCII-clean) instead of the non-ASCII '§3' character. EA-4 caught and fixed this transient regression themselves before reporting." NOT "EA-4 → EA-5 transition" and NOT "caught by Guide-#5 byte-level diff at v4 quality review."
error.correction_required: Replace footnote 7 with: "During EA-4's Item 2.5 PS1 corrections cycle (specifically la_merge_approve.ps1 + agents-cadence-monitor.ps1 McpConfigPath comment edits), a transient UTF-8-BOM encoding regression was inadvertently introduced. EA-4 self-caught and fixed in the same iteration before Guide-#5 review (commended at Guide-#5 disposition file `2.4-GUIDE_RESPONSE_INSTANCE_5_F1_AND_2.5_CORRECTIONS_APPROVED.xml`, check id='encoding_regression_self_caught'). No code-behavior change; cosmetic only."

error.ea_6_text: "Guide-#5 disposition g5-4: at Item 2.4 EA-3 originally implemented `LiveVikunjaClient` updates per-call rather than centralized via a new `update_task` method. Guide-#5 proposed two completion paths: Option A (hoist `update_task` into `LiveVikunjaClient` + migrate 6 callers) vs Option B (defer to Stage 3). LA selected Option A. Implemented at EA-5 → 6 zero-arg `LiveVikunjaClient` callers migrated → g5-4 SATISFIED at Item 2.7. Now in `3d1ac35` baseline."
error.actual:
g5-4 (per Guide-#5 disposition file 2.4-GUIDE_RESPONSE_INSTANCE_5_VERIFICATION_OF_EA_2.4_PARTIAL.xml required_acknowledgments_for_next_ea g5-4): about ctx-threading hazard in legacy `tools/fleet_observability/_vikunja_client.py:28` zero-arg `config_loader.load_config()` call. The hazard: post-Stage-4 cutover from devplatform cwd, the auto-resolve walks cwd and resolves to wrong overlay. Disposition path: ensure the line-28 callsite is closed before Item 2.9 commit.
EA-5 made the load_config conditional (D2-PARTIAL) at v5; EA-6 closed at Item 2.6 D2-completion via Option A AUTHORIZED by Guide-#6 (2.6-GUIDE_RESPONSE_INSTANCE_6_CFG_RESOLUTION_DIVERGENCE_DISPOSITION_OPTION_A_HOIST.xml): HOIST `cfg = config_loader.load_config(...)` block above the `LiveVikunjaClient` constructor in 3 divergent files (daily_digest, weekly_summary, run_live), and apply AFTER pattern (`base_url=cfg["vikunja"]["base_url"]`) to all 6 callers.
EA-6's text CONFLATES g5-4 (D2-completion ctx-threading; about config_loader and base_url passing) with D3 (dashboard_maintainer.py:113 private-method abuse via live._request → live.update_task closure). They are DISTINCT dispositions: - g5-4 / D2-completion: legacy fleet_observability/_vikunja_client.py line-28 zero-arg load_config callsite; closed via Option A hoist + base_url-passing in 6 callers. - D3: dashboard_maintainer.py:113 live._request("POST", ...) abuse; closed via new public `update_task` method on LiveVikunjaClient + caller rewrite. EA-6's narrative blends these as if g5-4 were about the update_task method. It isn't.
error.correction_required:
Replace footnote 11 with: "Guide-#5 disposition g5-4 (file `2.4-GUIDE_RESPONSE_INSTANCE_5_VERIFICATION_OF_EA_2.4_PARTIAL.xml`): legacy `tools/fleet_observability/_vikunja_client.py:28` had a zero-arg `config_loader.load_config()` call that would auto-resolve to the wrong project overlay post-Stage-4 cutover when invoked from devplatform cwd. EA-5 made the load_config conditional at v5 (D2-PARTIAL). EA-6 closed at Item 2.6 D2-completion via Guide-#6 Option A AUTHORIZED disposition (file `2.6-GUIDE_RESPONSE_INSTANCE_6_CFG_RESOLUTION_DIVERGENCE_DISPOSITION_OPTION_A_HOIST.xml`): hoisted `cfg = config_loader.load_config(...)` above the LiveVikunjaClient constructor in 3 divergent files (daily_digest, weekly_summary, run_live); applied AFTER pattern `base_url=cfg['vikunja']['base_url']` uniformly across all 6 callers (3 client + 3 live; assignee-naming preserved per r1). g5-4 SATISFIED at Item 2.7 per Guide-#6 D2_COMPLETION_VERIFIED file. Now in `3d1ac35` baseline.
(NOTE: D3 is a SEPARATE disposition -- dashboard_maintainer.py:113 private-method `live._request` abuse closed via new public `update_task` method on LiveVikunjaClient. Both g5-4 and D3 landed at EA-6's D2/D3/D4 cycle but they are distinct dispositions targeting distinct files.)"

error.ea_6_text: "g6-ea6_n7 — D1 NO-ACTION dispositions: Two findings during 2.7.v2 V matrix authoring (F-D1-1 MCP-bus query gap; F-D1-2 openvino harness fixture stale path) classified D1 (out-of-scope-for-Stage-2). NO-ACTION at Stage 2; both forwarded to Stage 4 backlog for re-evaluation post-cutover."
error.actual:
Per Guide-#6 disposition file `2.6-GUIDE_RESPONSE_INSTANCE_6_D1_NO_ACTION_DISPOSITIONS_AUTHORIZED.xml`: F-D1-1 and F-D1-2 surfaced during Item 2.6 D1 extended-grep verification net (12 patterns per Master Plan §6.2 + AUDIT_RISK_REVIEW C6), NOT during 2.7.v2 V matrix authoring.
F-D1-1 actual: vikunja_mcp/* MCP-server bus 19 hits across server.py + cli.py + bridge/daemon.py. Disposition: third chokepoint family parallel to chokepoint VikunjaClient + legacy LiveVikunjaClient; §D.1 enforcement preserved at function- level (D4 DIVERGE) + at user/protocol-input boundary (cli.py + bridge/daemon.py). Stage 5+ followup ticket suggested for VikunjaClient class-method routing migration (post-HTTP-delegation).
F-D1-2 actual: tools/openvino_contrib_agent/repo_tools.py uses urllib + Authorization Bearer for GitHub API (PAT-authenticated). Out-of-domain (GitHub, not Vikunja). Stage 5.1 keep_in_blarai list preserves openvino_contrib_agent in BlarAI permanently. NO-ACTION; no Stage 6 followup.
Forwarded to Stage 5+ (NOT "Stage 4 backlog" as EA-6 wrote). Stage 4 has its own scope; F-D1-1 + F-D1-2 are post-Stage-4 followups for VikunjaClient HTTP delegation (Stage 5+) or BlarAI-product-side concerns (out of platform-extraction scope).
error.correction_required:
Replace footnote 12.g6-ea6_n7 with the verbatim text from g6-ea6_n7 ack in the Guide-#6 disposition file `2.6-GUIDE_RESPONSE_INSTANCE_6_D1_NO_ACTION_DISPOSITIONS_AUTHORIZED.xml`:
"g6-ea6_n7 — D1 NO-ACTION dispositions (file `2.6-GUIDE_RESPONSE_INSTANCE_6_D1_NO_ACTION_DISPOSITIONS_AUTHORIZED.xml`): Item 2.6 D1 extended-grep verification net (12 patterns) surfaced two finding families outside the chokepoint-internal/legacy-shim-internal/test-only triage rule. Both authorized as NO-ACTION: - F-D1-1: vikunja_mcp/ MCP-bus, 19 hits across server.py + cli.py + bridge/daemon.py. Acknowledged as third chokepoint family parallel to (a) tools/_vikunja_client.VikunjaClient for fleet-runtime scripts and (b) tools/fleet_observability/_vikunja_client.LiveVikunjaClient legacy. §D.1 enforcement preserved at function-level (server.py D4 DIVERGE-APPROVED: 7 _require_project_id sites at handler entry per docstring fix at 2.7.v2) + at user/protocol-input level (cli.py CLI flags; bridge/daemon.py inbox protocol). Class-method routing through VikunjaClient deferred to Stage 5+ when full chokepoint HTTP delegation is wired. Stage 6.7.5-pattern DevPlatform-Meta hardening ticket suggested. - F-D1-2: openvino_contrib_agent (urllib + GitHub PAT for GitHub API; not Vikunja). Out-of-domain by definition; Stage 5.1 keep_in_blarai list preserves openvino_contrib_agent in BlarAI permanently. No Stage 6 followup."

error.topic: V matrix V4 vs closure V4 namespace conflation
error.ea_6_text: "g6-ea6_n8 — V4 vikunja-process drift Option B: V4 V matrix test attempted to start Vikunja under test-fixture control; conflicted with the host-running Vikunja serving the LA's parallel workflow. Option A (stop host Vikunja for tests) vs Option B (skip host-process management in V4, document drift as procedural). LA selected Option B per parallel-workflow priority. V4 retains 12 cases against in-process state stubs; live-process integration deferred to Stage 4.0/4.1 preflight."
error.actual:
Per Guide-#6 disposition file `2.6-GUIDE_RESPONSE_INSTANCE_6_V4_VIKUNJA_PROCESS_DRIFT_OPTION_B_AUTHORIZED.xml`: the V4 in g6-ea6_n8 is the V1-V7 CLOSURE assertion V4 (vikunja_processes = 0 host-state check from EA_HANDOFF_STAGE2.xml), NOT the V matrix V4 (CLI flag exposure parametrized per refactored entrypoint at 2.7.v2).
EA-6 themselves explicitly clarified the namespace at Item 2.7 closure: "V matrix V4 vs closure V4 namespace clarification noted: V matrix V4 = 'CLI flag exposure parametrized per refactored entrypoint' (NEW pytest test at 2.7.v2); closure V4 = 'vikunja processes = 0' (host-state check). Will be clarified in 2.10 STATUS append narrative." Footnote 12.g6-ea6_n8 was supposed to clarify this distinction; instead it CONFLATES the two namespaces (the V matrix V4 doesn't start Vikunja under test-fixture control -- it's pure subprocess --help introspection over 6 argparse entrypoints).
Actual g6-ea6_n8 disposition: at Item 2.7 V1-V7 closure assertion run, V4 returned 1 (vikunja-v2.3.0-windows-4.0-amd64.exe running on host) when expected 0. EA-6 STOP-and-reported with 3 options. Guide-#6 disposed Option B (STATUS-staleness rule 5b(a); LA's parallel-workflow Vikunja usage; Stage 4.1 preflight handles V4=0 restoration). NO mention of V matrix V4 starting Vikunja under test-fixture control -- that's a fictitious framing.
This error REVERSES the namespace clarification I authorized at Item 2.7 closure. Future readers of STATUS.md would think the V matrix V4 test is about Vikunja process management, which it ISN'T.
error.correction_required:
Replace footnote 12.g6-ea6_n8 with the verbatim text from g6-ea6_n8 ack in Guide-#6 disposition file `2.6-GUIDE_RESPONSE_INSTANCE_6_V4_VIKUNJA_PROCESS_DRIFT_OPTION_B_AUTHORIZED.xml`:
"g6-ea6_n8 — V1-V7 closure V4 (vikunja_processes = 0) drift, Option B AUTHORIZED: At Item 2.7 V1-V7 closure assertion run, V4 returned 1 (one `vikunja-v2.3.0-windows-4.0-amd64.exe` process running on host, PID 18304) where the V1-V7 closure assertion expected 0. EA-6 STOP-and-reported per Guide-#6 stop condition. Disposition: Option B AUTHORIZED -- V4=1 is STATUS-staleness per Guide-#6 operating rule 5b(a), NOT procedure breakage. The V4=0 invariant came from Stage 1.4 step (e) closure (Vikunja stopped after MCP project-creation to restore Stage-0.4 quiesced state). The LA's operating environment has reasons to keep Vikunja running during the multi-day Stage 2 work cycle (parallel Guide/Co-Lead chats; manual webUI ticket work). Stage 2 XML does NOT require Vikunja stopped; Stage 2 work is pure code refactor (no Vikunja DB access). Stage 4 EA's preflight at item 4.1 has explicit fail-fast guard for V4=1 ('if (\$running) { throw \"Vikunja is still running. Stop it before migrating the DB.\" }'), so V4=0 will be enforced at Stage 4 cutover. Stage 2 close proceeds with V4=1 acknowledged. Disposition source: Guide-#6 review file `2.6-GUIDE_RESPONSE_INSTANCE_6_V4_VIKUNJA_PROCESS_DRIFT_OPTION_B_AUTHORIZED.xml`.
(NAMESPACE NOTE: The V4 in this g6-ea6_n8 disposition is the V1-V7 CLOSURE assertion V4 from EA_HANDOFF_STAGE2.xml -- a host-state check on vikunja_processes count. It is DISTINCT from the V matrix V4 in 2.7.v2, which is 'CLI flag exposure parametrized per refactored entrypoint' -- a NEW pytest test with 12 cases at tools/tests/test_v_matrix_v4_cli_flag.py invoking subprocess --help over 6 argparse entrypoints. The two V4s share a label across distinct test/check namespaces; they are unrelated.)"

guide_review_response.required_corrections_summary:
EA-6 must apply 5 narrative corrections to STATUS.md "Stage 2 Closure Footnotes" section in a follow-on 2.10.1 commit. Specific edits: - Footnote 2 (g5-7): correct file name + author per N1 above - Footnote 7 (encoding regression): correct timing per N2 above - Footnote 11 (g5-4 + Option A + D2-completion): un-conflate g5-4 from D3 per N3 above - Footnote 12.g6-ea6_n7 (D1 NO-ACTION): correct stage timing + restore verbatim ack text per N4 above - Footnote 12.g6-ea6_n8 (V4 vikunja-process drift): UN-CONFLATE V matrix V4 from closure V4 (the most consequential correction); restore verbatim ack text + explicit namespace note per N5 above
The 2.10.1 commit message should be: "docs(platform_separation): Stage 2 closure footnote narrative corrections (Item 2.10.1)
Per Guide-#6 verdict file docs/platform_separation/temp_for_responses/2.6-GUIDE_RESPONSE_INSTANCE_6_ITEM_2_10_VERIFIED_NARRATIVE_CORRECTIONS_AUTHORIZED.xml: apply 5 narrative corrections to STATUS.md Stage 2 Closure Footnotes section. Footnotes 2, 7, 11, 12.g6-ea6_n7, 12.g6-ea6_n8 corrected to match verbatim disposition record in temp_for_responses/. Mechanical 2.10 commit (89213ee) is otherwise clean (parent 3d1ac35; +59/-5 STATUS.md; Guide-#6 verdict XML archive); this 2.10.1 corrects only the footnote narrative accuracy. No code change; no Stage Ledger / Anomaly / Recovery Anchors / Vikunja Project IDs / Execution Log change."
NO --amend per the no-amend doctrine (89213ee is already in git history; --amend would rewrite history). NEW commit (2.10.1) is the correct pattern.
After 2.10.1 lands successfully, EA-6 emits the closing handoff to LA in chat per the closing_handoff_to_la_at_stage_2_close block in the EA-6 init pointer.

ack_status_update.ack: The STATUS-append component is partially landed at 89213ee (footnote 12.g6-ea6_n9 narrative is brief but acceptably accurate per N inventory above; it's the OTHER footnotes that need correction, not g6-ea6_n9 itself). Full SATISFIED transition after 2.10.1 lands with all 5 narrative corrections.

NEW ack: 5 narrative corrections to STATUS.md Stage 2 Closure Footnotes section per <narrative_error_inventory> above. EA-6 acknowledges + applies as 2.10.1 follow-on commit before emitting closing handoff. Status transitions to SATISFIED at 2.10.1 commit landing.

commendations_for_ea_6.commendation: Item 2.10's most invariant-stress requirement is "append-only" -- existing entries MUST NOT be modified. EA-6 succeeded: A1-A10 anomalies untouched (lines 113-184 verbatim from prior STATUS); Stage 0/1 ledger rows untouched; Recovery Anchors section untouched; Vikunja Project IDs section untouched; prior Execution Log entries untouched; How to Resume After Interruption untouched. The only authorized edits were the Current State table (LIVING STATE per dn1) and the Stage 2 Stage Ledger row (PENDING → COMPLETE). Pure-append work of A11 anomaly + Stage 2 Closure Footnotes section + 2 new Execution Log entries.

stash@{0} timestamp 20260424_013002 byte-identical from v3 emission (2026-04-25) through every subsequent cycle including Item 2.10 close (2026-04-26): v3 → v4 → Guide-5/6 → v5 → 2.7 → 2.7.v2 → docstring/2.8 → 2.9 → 2.10. 10 cycles of restraint on a single stash entry. Cleanest single-axis discipline win in the procedure.

commendation.topic: Guide-#6 verdict XML archived in same commit per artifact guidance

commendations_for_ea_6.commendation: EA-6 included the Guide-#6 verdict XML file (2.6-GUIDE_RESPONSE_INSTANCE_6_ITEM_2_9_VERIFIED_ARTIFACT_GUIDANCE_PROCEED_TO_2_10.xml) in the 89213ee commit per the artifact-management guidance "KEEP audit chain in git" from the prior Guide-#6 verdict. Forward-staging the audit chain into the Stage 2 commit set rather than leaving it as a ?? entry indefinitely.

commendation.topic: Stage Ledger row evidence summary comprehensive

commendations_for_ea_6.commendation: The Stage 2 Stage Ledger row entry (line 50 of STATUS.md) is comprehensive: lists chokepoint helpers + per-project allowlist + --project-root/--project-id flags + -BlarAIRoot env-fallback in 6 PS1 + LiveVikunjaClient.update_task new method + g5-4 SATISFIED + D4 DIVERGE 7 _require_project_id retained + 4 V matrix tests with per-test case counts + pytest baseline transition + i1-3 satisfaction + footnote pointer. Single-line summary covers the entire Stage 2 substantive scope.

required_acknowledgments.ack_to_emit_at_start_of_next_chat_turn: Per g6-ea6_n10: EA-6 acknowledges the 5 narrative-correction obligations (footnotes 2, 7, 11, 12.g6-ea6_n7, 12.g6-ea6_n8) per <required_corrections_summary> above and applies as 2.10.1 follow-on commit before emitting the closing handoff. The 2.10.1 commit message template is provided above. Verification post-2.10.1: git log --oneline -1 shows 2.10.1 at HEAD (parent 89213ee); STATUS.md content reflects all 5 corrections; working tree clean post-commit.

guide_review_response.closing_handoff_timing_guidance:
EA-6 asked: "shall I (a) emit closing handoff now in this turn, or (b) wait for LA to acknowledge the 2.10 commit first?"
Guide-#6 answer: NEITHER. Order: (1) LA forwards this Guide-#6 verdict to EA-6 (after reviewing the 5 narrative errors). (2) EA-6 applies 5 narrative corrections + commits 2.10.1. (3) EA-6 emits closing handoff in chat per closing_handoff_to_la_at_stage_2_close block in EA-6 init pointer (Stage 2 commit hash 3d1ac35; V1-V7 + V8 outputs; per-item V8; V matrix V4-V7 test counts + names; A11 anomaly; decision narrative on D2/D3/D4 + D2-completion + D1 + V4 + g6-ea6_n9 docstring fix + g6-ea6_n10 narrative corrections; invariant confirmations). (4) Stage 2 truly CLOSES.
The closing handoff narrative SHOULD reference the corrected footnotes (post-2.10.1). Emitting closing handoff before 2.10.1 lands risks the closing narrative inheriting the same 5 narrative errors -- the LA would have to ask for re-emission. Better to correct the source (STATUS.md) first; then the closing handoff narrative naturally reflects accurate dispositions.

foundation_solidity_check_per_la_motto.intro: The LA's recurring concern: "Is the work mature not minimal?" Foundation check at Item 2.10 close (immediately pre-closing-handoff):
foundation_solidity_check_per_la_motto.evidence:
Structural axes (PASSED): - 12-item cumulative footnote SET present in STATUS.md (per ack g5-10 + g6-ea6_n7/n8/n9) - Stage Ledger row Stage 2 PENDING → COMPLETE with comprehensive evidence summary - A11 anomaly appended with full disposition narrative - Append-only discipline preserved (A1-A10 / Stage 0/1 / Recovery / Vikunja IDs all untouched) - 10-cycle stash byte-identity preserved - state.json invariants verbatim through 2.5+ days - 2.10 commit mechanically clean (parent 3d1ac35; +59/-5 STATUS.md; Guide-#6 verdict XML archived) - Working tree clean post-commit
Narrative-accuracy axis (FAILS-AT-FIVE-FOOTNOTES): - Footnote 2 (g5-7): wrong file name + wrong EA author -- LOW-MED severity - Footnote 7 (encoding regression): wrong session timing -- LOW severity - Footnote 11 (g5-4 + Option A): conflates g5-4 with D3 -- HIGH severity - Footnote 12.g6-ea6_n7: wrong stage timing -- MED severity - Footnote 12.g6-ea6_n8: V matrix V4 vs closure V4 namespace conflation -- HIGH severity (reverses the explicit clarification I noted at Item 2.7 close)
foundation_solidity_check_per_la_motto.verdict:
Stage 2 substantive work is COMPLETE (3d1ac35 baseline + 89213ee STATUS append). But Stage 2 audit-chain narrative accuracy is INCOMPLETE pending the 5 corrections via 2.10.1 follow-on commit.
EA-6 has met "mature not minimal" emphatically on the structural axes (commit mechanics + append-only discipline + invariant preservation through 10 cycles + 12-item footnote set presence + Guide-#6 verdict archival per artifact guidance). EA-6 falls short on the narrative-accuracy axis at the FOOTNOTE-NARRATIVE level specifically: 5 paragraphs of paraphrased disposition narratives that introduced factual errors during paraphrase. The corrections are easily applicable (replace paraphrase with verbatim text from disposition files). Once 2.10.1 lands, Stage 2 narrative axis matches the structural axis.
This is consistent with EA-6's track record: thoroughness on structure + occasional paraphrase-introduced errors that disk-verification catches. The discipline cuts both ways -- when EA-6 narrative-paraphrases, errors creep in (here at Item 2.10 + previously at the +1 M attribution drift in v5 XML + the "8 vs 7" docstring drift at file 13). The mature response is consistent: surface the errors via independent Guide-side disk verification + correct them via in-scope follow-on commits before claiming closure.
After 2.10.1 lands, Stage 2 will be SOLIDLY closed across both structural and narrative axes.

guide_review_response.proceed_authorization:
PROCEED -- in the next chat turn, EA-6 executes:
Step 1: Read this Guide-#6 verdict file in full to internalize the 5 narrative error inventory (N1-N5) and the corresponding correction texts. Step 2: Apply 5 narrative corrections to docs/platform_separation/STATUS.md "Stage 2 Closure Footnotes" section per <required_corrections_summary> (footnotes 2, 7, 11, 12.g6-ea6_n7, 12.g6-ea6_n8). Do NOT modify any other section of STATUS.md (Append-only discipline still applies; Anomaly A11, Stage Ledger row, Current State table, Execution Log entries, Recovery Anchors, Vikunja Project IDs, How to Resume should all be untouched in 2.10.1). Step 3: git add docs/platform_separation/STATUS.md; git commit with the 2.10.1 commit message template above. NO --amend (89213ee is in history; no-amend doctrine applies). Step 4: Verify post-commit: - git log --oneline -1: 2.10.1 at HEAD; parent 89213ee - git show --stat (latest): ONE file in diff (STATUS.md); modest line changes (~15-25 lines net since the corrections are paragraph replacements) - git status --porcelain: empty (clean working tree) - cat docs/platform_separation/STATUS.md: footnotes 2, 7, 11, 12.g6-ea6_n7, 12.g6-ea6_n8 reflect corrections - pytest tools/ --tb=short -q: 351 passed (no runtime impact; STATUS.md edit is docs-only) Step 5: Emit closing handoff to LA in chat per closing_handoff_to_la_at_stage_2_close block in EA-6 init pointer. Reference the corrected footnotes.
Stop conditions during 2.10.1: - If the corrections inadvertently modify other STATUS.md sections (Anomalies, Stage Ledger, Recovery Anchors, etc.), STOP and revert -- the 2.10.1 commit scope is footnote narrative corrections ONLY. - If pytest baseline regresses below 351, STOP and investigate (it shouldn't; the edits are documentation-only). - If git status post-commit shows non-empty, investigate before declaring 2.10.1 closed.
Closing handoff timing: emit AFTER 2.10.1 lands; reference the corrected footnotes in the decision narrative; finalize Stage 2 close with the clean audit chain.
Standing invariants unchanged: no further Stage 2 commits beyond 2.10.1; fleet stays paused (NEVER state.resume_fleet); no merge to main; stash@{0} untouched (will become 11-cycle preservation after 2.10.1 lands).
After Stage 2 truly closes, EA-6 retires; the procedure transitions to Stage 3 (a separate fresh-EA spawn under a new Guide instance or this Guide-#6 if context permits).
